title: NuScale DCA Tier 2 — Ch.6.2.3 Passive Containment Heat Removal
text: The NuScale Passive Containment Cooling System (PCCS) removes decay heat from the steel Containment Vessel (CNV) by submerging it in the Reactor Pool. Heat transfer occurs through the CNV wall via condensation on the inner surface and pool-side natural convection on the outer surface. No AC power or operator action is required for at least 72 hours. PCT remains below 1204 degC under DBA conditions per 10 CFR 50.46.

title: NuScale DCA Tier 2 — Ch.6.2.3 PCS Heat Sink Performance
text: PCS heat sink performance is evaluated assuming the Reactor Pool inventory remains adequate for 72 hours without makeup. Sensitivity studies with reduced pool inventory (-30%) and degraded pool boiling at the CNV outer surface confirm peak containment pressure stays below the 1000 psia design limit. The CNV is vacuum-rated (~0.1 psia internal) which suppresses convective heat transfer pre-event and improves post-event condensation.

title: NuScale DCA Tier 2 — Ch.5.4.1 Reactor Coolant System Design (Helical Coil SG)
text: The NuScale Power Module integrates two helical coil steam generators (SG) inside the Reactor Pressure Vessel. Primary coolant flows downward on the shell side of the helical tube bundle; secondary fluid flows upward inside the tubes and exits as superheated steam at ~302 degC. Tube material is Alloy 690TT. No reactor coolant pumps are used; primary flow is driven entirely by natural circulation.

title: NuScale DCA Tier 2 — Ch.4.4 Thermal-Hydraulic Design
text: Natural circulation primary flow delivers 200 MWt thermal removal at full power. Driving head is the density difference between the hot riser and the downcomer surrounding the helical SG. NRELAP5 best-estimate code with quantified uncertainty (95/95 per RG 1.157) is used. Minimum DNBR margin and PCT remain bounded across the operating envelope; no flow instability is observed in steady state.

title: NuScale DCA Tier 2 — Ch.4.4.4 Flow Stability — Density Wave Oscillation
text: Density Wave Oscillation (DWO) on the secondary side of the helical SG is identified as a potential instability mode unique to the NuScale natural-circulation, once-through, superheated steam design. Stability margin is quantified by the decay ratio (DR); acceptance criterion is DR < 0.5 across 100% of the operating envelope including startup, low-power, and full-power. Methodology and qualification basis are documented in Topical Report TR-0516-49416-P (NRELAP5) and TR-0915-17564-P (DWO stability).

title: NuScale DCA Tier 2 — Ch.15.6.5 Loss-of-Coolant Accident
text: LOCA spectrum analysis for the NuScale Power Module covers Reactor Vent Valve (RVV), Reactor Recirculation Valve (RRV), and CVCS line breaks. ECCS actuation opens the RVVs/RRVs to depressurize the RPV into the CNV; the CNV then transfers heat to the Reactor Pool via PCS. PCT remains below 1204 degC (2200 degF) with >300 degF margin demonstrated in DCA Chapter 15.6.5.

title: NuScale DCA Tier 2 — Ch.7 Module Protection System (MPS)
text: The NuScale Module Protection System (MPS) is a four-division safety-related digital I&C platform (HIPS, Highly Integrated Protection System) providing reactor trip and ESFAS. Diversity and Defense-in-Depth (D3) is achieved via a non-safety diverse actuation system. Cyber security is addressed per RG 5.71. The MPS satisfies the single-failure criterion of 10 CFR 50 Appendix A GDC 21.

title: NuScale Topical Report TR-0915-17564-P — DWO Stability Methodology
text: DWO stability methodology applied to the NuScale helical coil SG uses time-domain transient simulation with NRELAP5, followed by autoregressive (AR) decay-ratio extraction from a small flow perturbation. Stability margin is mapped on a power-flow operating map. The methodology is qualified against single-tube DWO experiments at the SIET TF-1 facility and full-bundle scaled tests at the TF-2 facility.

title: NuScale Topical Report TR-0915-17564-P — TF-1 Single-Tube Test Results
text: The TF-1 facility at SIET (Piacenza, Italy) is a full-height, full-pressure single helical tube replicating the NuScale SG hydraulic boundary. Across 184 test points (P=3.45–10 MPa, mass flux 200–800 kg/m2s, inlet subcooling 0–40 K), measured DRs ranged 0.05–0.42. NRELAP5 predictions are within ±0.10 absolute DR at 95% of points, supporting use as a best-estimate stability code per RG 1.203 EMDAP.

title: NuScale Topical Report TR-0915-17564-P — TF-2 Multi-Tube Bundle Tests
text: The TF-2 facility extends TF-1 to a 7-tube scaled bundle to capture parallel-channel DWO effects (out-of-phase oscillation between tubes). TF-2 measured an additional ~0.05 DR penalty vs. single-tube at low-power conditions due to inter-tube coupling. The penalty is applied as a conservative bias in the NuScale DWO acceptance criterion (DR_apparent + 0.05 ≤ 0.5).

title: NuScale Topical Report TR-0915-17564-P — DWO Limitations and Open Items
text: Acknowledged limitations of the DWO qualification: (1) TF-1/TF-2 use water-only loops, not prototypic secondary-side carryover; (2) startup transients with feedwater temperature ramping are bounded analytically but not directly tested; (3) long-term tube fouling effects on flow resistance are addressed by sensitivity studies, not by experiment. NuScale commits to operational monitoring of feedwater flow oscillation amplitude as a defense-in-depth measure.

title: NuScale Topical Report TR-0316-22048 — Helical SG Tube Structural Integrity
text: Structural integrity evaluation of the helical SG tube under postulated DWO oscillations follows ASME Section III, NB-3200 high-cycle fatigue criteria. Pressure and temperature oscillation amplitudes corresponding to DR=0.5 are propagated to tube stress; cumulative usage factor (CUF) remains below 0.1 over a 60-year design life. Flow-induced vibration (FIV) wear at tube supports is evaluated per ASME OM Part 3.

title: NRC RAI 8916 — DWO Stability Demonstration (Question)
text: RAI 8916 Question 1: Justify the adequacy of the DWO stability assessment for the helical coil SG. Specifically: (a) demonstrate that the decay ratio acceptance criterion of 0.5 is conservative relative to the NRC-accepted 0.8 for BWR thermal-hydraulic stability per GL 94-02; (b) provide separate-effects test data supporting parallel-channel coupling treatment; (c) address sensitivity to inlet flow distribution among tubes. Regulatory basis: 10 CFR 50.46, GDC 10, RG 1.203 EMDAP Element 4 (assess adequacy).

title: NuScale RAI 8916 Response — DWO Stability (Response)
text: NuScale Response to RAI 8916: (a) the 0.5 DR criterion is tighter than the 0.8 BWR limit because the helical coil SG operates in superheated regime where small DWO can cause large temperature swings; conservatism is justified by structural fatigue considerations [TR-0316-22048]; (b) TF-2 multi-tube data quantifies parallel-channel penalty (+0.05 DR); (c) flow distribution sensitivity is bounded by ±10% inlet maldistribution case showing DR increase <0.03. References: TR-0915-17564-P Sections 5–6.

title: NRC RAI 9013 — Helical SG Tube Structural Integrity under DWO (Question)
text: RAI 9013 Question 2: For DWO conditions at the upper-bound decay ratio, demonstrate that helical coil SG tube cumulative usage factor (CUF) and flow-induced vibration (FIV) wear satisfy ASME Section III NB-3200 and OM Part 3 acceptance. Provide the assumed oscillation amplitude, frequency spectrum, and number of cycles over plant life. Regulatory basis: GDC 14, GDC 31, 10 CFR 50.55a; SRP 5.4.1.1 Acceptance Criterion II.2.

title: NuScale RAI 9013 Response — Helical SG Tube Integrity (Response)
text: NuScale Response to RAI 9013: Bounding DWO amplitude assumed = 10% of nominal mass flux at 0.5–2 Hz (envelopes TF-1/TF-2 observations). Total cycles over 60-yr life = 1.4E7. ASME NB-3200 fatigue evaluation yields CUF = 0.072 (limit 1.0). FIV wear at tube supports computed per Connors correlation; max wear depth = 0.18 mm (limit 0.50 mm, 36% wall thickness). Reference: TR-0316-22048 Section 4.4.

title: NRC RAI 9588 — PCS Degraded Pool Performance (Question)
text: RAI 9588: Address the 72-hour passive containment heat removal claim under degraded Reactor Pool conditions including reduced inventory (boiloff) and degraded pool-side heat transfer (subcooled boiling at the CNV outer wall). Regulatory basis: GDC 38, GDC 39, 10 CFR 50.46(b)(5), SRP 6.2.2.

title: NuScale RAI 9588 Response — PCS Heat Removal (Response)
text: NuScale Response to RAI 9588: Sensitivity case with 30% reduced Reactor Pool inventory and Chen-correlation pool-side heat transfer shows peak containment pressure 893 psia at 72 h vs. 1000 psia design limit. Post-72h passive performance is supported by a non-safety pool makeup line. Cited section: FSAR 6.2.3.2; supplemental data in calculation NS-ER-PCS-007.

title: NRC RAI 9573 — ECCS Valve Single Failure (Question)
text: RAI 9573: Demonstrate ECCS valve actuation under postulated single failure of an inadvertent actuation block (IAB) valve. Regulatory basis: GDC 35, 10 CFR 50.46, SRP 6.3 Acceptance Criterion II.3.

title: NuScale RAI 9573 Response — ECCS Single Failure (Response)
text: NuScale Response to RAI 9573: 2-of-3 RVV / 2-of-2 RRV opening required for ECCS function. Single failure of one IAB latch in any one RVV is tolerated; analysis shows PCT remains below 1204 degC with one RVV unavailable. Reference: FSAR 15.6.5, Table 6.3-2.

title: NRC RAI 9302 — Source Term and Control Room Habitability (Question)
text: RAI 9302: Justify the alternative source term applied to NuScale dose consequence analyses; address the absence of a steam-driven Main Steam Isolation Valve (MSIV) closure signal under specific event sequences. Regulatory basis: RG 1.183, 10 CFR 50.67, SRP 15.0.3.

title: NuScale RAI 9302 Response — Source Term (Response)
text: NuScale Response to RAI 9302: Source term per RG 1.183 with modifications justified by smaller core (160 MWt) and integral primary; cesium release fraction reduced from 0.25 to 0.10 supported by VEGA/PHEBUS data review. MSIV closure on low-low main steam pressure and high containment pressure ensures isolation within 5 s. Cited: FSAR 15.0.3, NS-TR-2018-014.

title: NRC RAI 9745 — Digital I&C Diversity (Question)
text: RAI 9745: Provide the diversity and defense-in-depth (D3) assessment for the HIPS-based MPS against common-cause software failure. Regulatory basis: BTP 7-19, SRP 7.0, 10 CFR 50.62, 10 CFR 50 App A GDC 22.

title: NuScale RAI 9745 Response — D3 Assessment (Response)
text: NuScale Response to RAI 9745: A non-safety, FPGA-based diverse actuation system provides reactor trip and ECCS actuation on diverse parameters (containment pressure, level). Two-out-of-four divisional voting on MPS plus diverse system satisfies BTP 7-19 echelon-of-defense. Cited: FSAR 7.8.

title: NRC RAI 9210 — Containment Leak Rate Testing (Question)
text: RAI 9210: Justify the omission of Type A integrated leak rate testing for the small, vacuum-rated NuScale CNV. Regulatory basis: 10 CFR 50 App J, RG 1.163, SRP 6.2.6.

title: NuScale RAI 9210 Response — Containment Leak Test (Response)
text: NuScale Response to RAI 9210: Vacuum-rated CNV permits direct vacuum-decay leak-rate measurement at every fuel cycle, providing higher sensitivity than App J Type A test. Exemption from Type A is requested; Type B/C continues for penetrations. Cited: FSAR 6.2.6, Exemption Request NS-EXM-006.

title: NRC RAI 9450 — Severe Accident / ISLOCA (Question)
text: RAI 9450: Provide the ISLOCA frequency analysis for CVCS letdown and Decay Heat Removal interfacing piping; address the absence of high-pressure containment isolation valves on these lines. Regulatory basis: 10 CFR 50.34(f), SECY-93-087, SRP 19.0.

title: NuScale RAI 9450 Response — ISLOCA (Response)
text: NuScale Response to RAI 9450: PRA-Level 1 ISLOCA frequency = 2.3E-9 /reactor-yr, dominated by CVCS letdown line. Two parallel containment isolation valves with diverse actuation are credited; LOCA outside containment is bounded by the design basis LOCA dose consequence. Cited: FSAR 19.1, PRA-2018.

title: NRC RAI 9881 — Boron Dilution under Natural Circulation (Question)
text: RAI 9881: Address the boron dilution event scenarios specific to natural circulation primary flow, including unborated slug formation in the helical SG riser region. Regulatory basis: GDC 26, SRP 15.4.6, RG 1.70.

title: NuScale RAI 9881 Response — Boron Dilution (Response)
text: NuScale Response to RAI 9881: CVCS makeup line geometry precludes injection of unborated water above the core. NRELAP5 mixing analysis shows minimum boron concentration in the downcomer stays above the critical-boron limit by >300 ppm. Cited: FSAR 15.4.6.

title: NRC SER NUREG-2257 — Ch.6.2 Containment Systems Evaluation
text: Staff Evaluation (NUREG-2257 §6.2.3): The NRC staff concludes that the NuScale PCS meets GDC 38 and 10 CFR 50.46(b)(5) for 72-hour passive heat removal. The vacuum-rated CNV with pool immersion is acceptable; the staff accepts the alternative leak-rate testing approach with the exemption from App J Type A (FSAR 6.2.6). Open item closed by RAI 9588 and 9210 responses.

title: NRC SER NUREG-2257 — Ch.4.4 Stability and DWO Evaluation
text: Staff Evaluation (NUREG-2257 §4.4.4): The decay-ratio acceptance criterion DR<0.5, combined with the +0.05 multi-tube penalty derived from TF-2, provides reasonable conservatism vs. GL 94-02. The staff finds NRELAP5 acceptable as a stability code under RG 1.203 EMDAP. Open items from RAI 8916 and 9013 are closed; staff imposes a license condition requiring operational DWO monitoring per FSAR Tech Spec 3.4.X.

title: NRC SER NUREG-2257 — Ch.7 Instrumentation and Controls
text: Staff Evaluation (NUREG-2257 §7): The HIPS platform with D3 diverse actuation satisfies BTP 7-19. Cyber security per RG 5.71 acceptable. Two open items on FPGA tooling qualification closed by NuScale supplemental submittal NS-TR-2019-021.

title: NRC SER NUREG-2257 — Ch.15 Accident Analysis
text: Staff Evaluation (NUREG-2257 §15.6.5): Spectrum of LOCA events analyzed using NRELAP5 with RG 1.157 95/95 uncertainty methodology. PCT margin >300 degF accepted by staff. Single-failure analysis (RAI 9573 closure) acceptable. ATWS analysis under FSAR 15.8 likewise accepted.

title: NRC Audit Report 2018-04 — DWO Calculation Audit (NuScale Corvallis Office)
text: Audit team reviewed NRELAP5 DWO calculation set NS-CALC-DWO-007. Spot-check of 12 cases reproduced reported decay ratios within ±0.02. Configuration management of NRELAP5 source revisions adequate. Two minor observations: (1) one input deck used an older property table than baseline (no impact); (2) one sensitivity case folder lacked the standard QA sign-off page (corrected in audit closure).

title: NRC Audit Report 2017-09 — Containment Thermal-Hydraulic Audit
text: Audit reviewed PCS heat removal calculations supporting RAI 9588 response. Reactor Pool boiloff model uses Zuber-Findlay correlation; staff accepts. Audit recommended an additional sensitivity on pool-side surface fouling — closed by FSAR 6.2.3.2 Rev.5 update.

title: NuScale Licensing Issue Category Summary (Aggregated from SER+RAI corpus)
text: Aggregate NuScale licensing issue categories (counts approximate, SER NUREG-2257 + ~280 RAI letters publicly indexed): Safety Systems & Passive Cooling 18% (PCS, DHRS, ECCS valves); Accident Analysis 17% (LOCA, non-LOCA, ATWS, source term); Thermal-Hydraulics & Stability 14% (DWO, natural circulation, NRELAP5 qualification); Instrumentation & Controls 13% (HIPS, D3, cyber, FPGA); Structural/Materials 11% (helical SG tubes, RPV, fatigue/FIV); Containment & Leak Testing 9% (vacuum CNV, App J, isolation); Severe Accident & PRA 7% (ISLOCA, Level 2); Radiological / Source Term 5% (alternative source term, dose); Human Factors / Control Room 3%; Other (siting, EP) 3%.

title: NuScale Licensing Issue — Category Representative Examples
text: Category representatives: Safety Systems → RAI 9588 (PCS degraded pool), RAI 9573 (ECCS single failure); Accident Analysis → RAI 9302 (source term), RAI 9881 (boron dilution); T-H & Stability → RAI 8916 (DWO decay ratio), RAI 9013 (helical SG fatigue under DWO); I&C → RAI 9745 (D3); Containment → RAI 9210 (App J exemption); Severe Accident → RAI 9450 (ISLOCA). DWO accounts for the largest single-topic RAI cluster (~8% of all RAIs).

title: NuScale Licensing — Most Frequent NRC RAI Topics (Ranked)
text: Top RAI topics by frequency (descending): (1) DWO stability & helical SG behavior; (2) Natural circulation T-H code (NRELAP5) qualification; (3) ECCS valve actuation & single failure; (4) PCS / containment heat removal under degraded pool; (5) HIPS digital I&C D3 and FPGA qualification; (6) Alternative source term & control room habitability; (7) Containment leak rate testing (App J exemption); (8) ISLOCA frequency; (9) Boron dilution under natural circulation; (10) MPS cyber security.

title: U.S. NRC Regulatory Guide 1.203 — Transient & Accident Analysis Methods (EMDAP)
text: RG 1.203 establishes the Evaluation Model Development and Assessment Process (EMDAP) — six elements: (1) specify EM requirements, (2) develop assessment base, (3) develop EM, (4) assess EM adequacy, (5) follow EM development plan, (6) document. Element 4 (adequacy assessment) requires comparison to applicable separate-effects and integral test data, with quantified uncertainty.

title: U.S. NRC Regulatory Guide 1.157 — Best-Estimate Calculations of ECCS Performance
text: RG 1.157 §4.2: Best-estimate ECCS calculations shall include a quantification of uncertainty. Total uncertainty determined with 95-percent probability at 95-percent confidence (95/95). PCT shall not exceed 2200 degF (1204 degC) under any postulated LOCA per 10 CFR 50.46(b)(1).

title: U.S. NRC Regulatory Guide 1.206 — Combined License Applications, Format & Content
text: RG 1.206 provides format and content guidance for COL/DC applications, organized in 19 chapters mirroring SRP NUREG-0800. Each FSAR chapter identifies the GDCs, regulations, and SRP acceptance criteria it addresses.

title: U.S. NRC Regulatory Guide 1.70 — Standard Format and Content of SARs (Ch.15)
text: RG 1.70 Ch.15 categorizes accidents in 8 event categories (increase in heat removal, decrease in heat removal, decrease/increase in flow, reactivity anomalies, increase/decrease in inventory, radioactive release, ATWS). The applicant must analyze a representative event in each category.

title: 10 CFR 50.46 — Acceptance Criteria for ECCS for Light-Water-Reactors
text: 10 CFR 50.46(b): (1) PCT ≤ 2200 degF; (2) maximum cladding oxidation ≤ 17% of pre-transient thickness; (3) maximum hydrogen generation ≤ 1% of clad metal-water reaction theoretical; (4) coolable geometry maintained; (5) long-term core cooling maintained.

title: 10 CFR 50 Appendix A — General Design Criteria 21 (Protection System Reliability)
text: GDC 21: The protection system shall be designed for high functional reliability and inservice testability commensurate with the safety functions to be performed. Redundancy and independence shall ensure that no single failure results in loss of protection function.

title: 10 CFR 50 Appendix A — GDC 35 (Emergency Core Cooling)
text: GDC 35: A system to provide abundant emergency core cooling shall be provided. The system safety function shall be to transfer heat from the reactor core following any LOCA at a rate such that fuel and clad damage that could interfere with continued effective core cooling is prevented. Suitable redundancy in components and features, plus single-failure tolerance and electric power independence (offsite + onsite), are required.

title: 10 CFR 50 Appendix A — GDC 38 (Containment Heat Removal)
text: GDC 38: A containment heat removal system shall be provided. Its safety function shall be to rapidly reduce, consistent with the functioning of other associated systems, containment pressure and temperature following any LOCA and maintain them at acceptably low levels.

title: NUREG-0800 SRP §4.4 — Thermal and Hydraulic Design (Stability)
text: SRP 4.4 Acceptance Criterion II.4 (Stability): The applicant shall demonstrate that thermal-hydraulic instability (including density wave oscillation) does not occur in any operating condition; or, if margin to instability is small, an instability detection and suppression system shall be provided. Acceptable decay-ratio criteria depend on reactor type; conservatism beyond GL 94-02 is encouraged for novel designs.

title: NUREG-0800 SRP §5.4.1.1 — PWR Steam Generator Design
text: SRP 5.4.1.1 Acceptance Criterion II.2: SG tubes shall meet ASME Section III, Subsection NB requirements for primary pressure boundary integrity including fatigue (CUF<1.0 over design life) and flow-induced vibration wear assessment.

title: NUREG-0800 SRP §6.2.6 — Containment Leakage Testing
text: SRP 6.2.6 references 10 CFR 50 Appendix J for containment leakage testing (Type A integrated, Type B penetrations, Type C valves). Alternative testing methods may be approved upon demonstration of equivalent or improved leakage detection capability.

title: NUREG-0800 BTP 7-19 — Diversity & Defense-in-Depth (D3) for Digital I&C
text: BTP 7-19 establishes echelons of defense for digital safety systems against common-cause failure: (1) control system, (2) reactor trip, (3) ESFAS, (4) monitoring. Diversity in software, hardware, signals, equipment, and human action is required across echelons.

title: NuScale Compliance Map — RG 1.203 & SRP 4.4 (DWO Stability)
text: Mapping: RG 1.203 EMDAP and SRP 4.4 stability criteria are addressed by NuScale via TR-0915-17564-P (DWO methodology), TR-0516-49416-P (NRELAP5), FSAR Ch.4.4.4, and RAI 8916/9013 responses. Acceptance: DR<0.5 criterion + TF-1/TF-2 qualification + Tech Spec 3.4.X monitoring. NRC closure documented in NUREG-2257 §4.4.4.

title: NuScale Compliance Map — 10 CFR 50.46 & RG 1.157 (ECCS Performance)
text: Mapping: 10 CFR 50.46(b)(1)–(5) and RG 1.157 95/95 are addressed by FSAR Ch.15.6.5 LOCA spectrum analysis using NRELAP5. Demonstrated PCT margin >300 degF; uncertainty quantification per RG 1.157. Single-failure tolerance verified via RAI 9573 response. SER NUREG-2257 §15.6.5 closes.

title: NuScale Compliance Map — GDC 38 & 10 CFR 50 App J (Containment)
text: Mapping: GDC 38 (containment heat removal) is satisfied by PCS via pool immersion (FSAR 6.2.3). 10 CFR 50 App J Type A is replaced by vacuum-decay testing under approved Exemption NS-EXM-006; Type B/C remain. NRC closure in NUREG-2257 §6.2.

title: NuScale Compliance Map — SRP 5.4.1.1 & ASME III (SG Tube Integrity)
text: Mapping: SRP 5.4.1.1 II.2 and ASME III NB-3200 are addressed by TR-0316-22048 (helical SG tube fatigue under DWO), with CUF=0.072 demonstrated. FIV wear via Connors correlation, max wear 36% wall (limit 40%). Tied to RAI 9013 response. SER NUREG-2257 §5.4.1 closure.

title: NuScale Compliance Map — BTP 7-19 & GDC 21/22 (Digital I&C D3)
text: Mapping: GDC 21/22 and BTP 7-19 D3 requirements addressed by HIPS MPS + non-safety FPGA-based diverse actuation (FSAR 7.2, 7.8). Cyber security per RG 5.71. Closure via RAI 9745 response; SER NUREG-2257 §7.

title: 원자력안전위원회고시 — 소형모듈원자로 설계기준 (KINS-RG-2024-001)
text: KINS-RG-2024-001 §3.3 — ECCS는 능동/피동 계통을 조합하여 사고 후 72시간 무인 잔열 제거를 보장해야 한다. 피동 안전계통의 신뢰도는 평균 1.0E-6/년 미만의 노심손상빈도(CDF)를 만족해야 한다. 자연순환 일차계통의 유동 안정성 평가를 요구한다.

title: IAEA SSR-2/1 — Safety of Nuclear Power Plants: Design (Defence in Depth)
text: IAEA SSR-2/1 Requirement 16: Defence in depth shall be applied to all safety-related activities. Five levels — prevention of abnormal operation; control of abnormal operation; control of accidents within DBA; control of severe plant conditions; mitigation of radiological consequences — apply equally to SMR designs.

title: 내부 설계사양서 — 일체형 PWR SMR (2025-Q1)
text: 피동 잔열제거계통(PRHRS)은 격납건물 내 응축수조와 자연대류 루프로 구성되며, 외부 동력 없이 7일 이상 잔열 제거가 가능하도록 설계되었다. 능동 보조급수계통(AFW)은 다양성 확보 목적으로 병행 설치한다. 일차계통은 자연순환 방식이며, 나선형 증기발생기를 채택한다.
